message:
Fix rate limiter atomicity bug, enrich streaming errors, remove client-side character limits, and migrate connector tests to tokio

Rate limiter (fcp-sdk): The multi-pool try_consume had a partial-commit
bug where pool A could be consumed even if pool B rejected the request.
Fixed by splitting into two phases: phase 1 checks all pools without
mutating (check_consume), phase 2 commits only if all hard limits pass.
Soft limits now use force_consume to record usage after logging the
warning. The test pool ordering was also corrected to verify that the
"loose" pool is NOT consumed when "tight" rejects.

Streaming (fcp-sdk): ReplayError::InvalidCursor and CursorStale now
include the topic name in both the error variant fields and the Display
format string, so callers can diagnose which topic had a stale or
unparseable cursor without additional context.

Discord connector: Removed three copies of the 2000-character
MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH validation (validate_input_early, invoke_send_message,
invoke_edit_message). Discord's API enforces this limit server-side and
returns a clear 400 error; client-side validation was preventing valid
use cases like Nitro users with higher limits.

Twitter connector: Removed client-side 280-character tweet length
validation from invoke_create_tweet and invoke_reply_tweet for the same
reason — the API enforces limits and returns actionable errors.

Connector tests: Migrated all integration test files from
#[fcp_async_core::runtime::test] back to #[tokio::test]. The connector
tests use wiremock/MockServer which requires a tokio runtime directly.
Added tokio dev-dependency to anthropic, github, gitlab, plaid, qdrant,
and telegram connector Cargo.toml files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
